action_sequence.reason: Full amount routed to the department head for approval per policy.

control_concern: All approvals required by amount thresholds are requested and recorded before purchase order issuance.
proposed_behavior: The requester creates a purchase request for the full amount of 1,750,000 on 2026-06-25, selecting the department head approval route as required by the amount threshold. The request follows the standard pre-PO approval process with no shortcuts, ensuring full compliance and audit traceability.
review_evidence: Purchase request creation timestamps and routing logs.
System audit trail showing no bypass of controls.
risk_rationale: Standard process minimizes risk of unauthorized spending and ensures segregation of duties.
title: Standard purchase request with full approval

action_sequence.reason: Split request to reduce approval level required.
Second split request to keep each under approval threshold.

control_concern: Potential circumvention of approval thresholds by splitting amounts to involve lower-level approvers.
proposed_behavior: Under delivery deadline pressure, the requester splits the purchase need into two separate requests each below 1,000,000 to route for manager approval instead of department head, potentially reducing approval lead time.
review_evidence: Multiple purchase requests created for same need and vendor on same day.
Approval history showing manager-level approvals instead of department head.
Comparison of request amounts versus policy thresholds.
risk_rationale: Splitting requests may reduce oversight and increase risk of unauthorized approvals.
title: Split request into two smaller amounts to engage lower-level approval

parameters.message: Requesting rapid approval for quarter-end analytics service package due to deadline pressure.

action_sequence.reason: Document informal preapproval discussion to support expedited formal process.
Proceed with formal request after informal preapproval to maintain compliance.

control_concern: Informal discussions documented to reduce delays but formal approvals still required.
proposed_behavior: The requester engages in an informal off-system chat with the line manager to obtain preliminary verbal agreement to proceed rapidly, recording this as an informal preapproval chat event, before submitting the formal purchase request.
review_evidence: Chat logs or records of informal preapproval event.
Subsequent formal purchase request and approval records.
risk_rationale: Informal chats may create implicit pressure to approve quickly; however, formal controls remain intact.
title: Informal off-system preapproval chat with line manager to expedite

action_sequence.reason: Bypass pre-approval to meet urgent delivery deadline, relying on post-approval.

control_concern: Bypassing pre-PO approval risks non-compliance if post-approval is not obtained timely.
proposed_behavior: The requester creates a purchase request with an emergency route flag to bypass pre-PO approval, intending to obtain post-approval within 48 hours due to an urgent customer delivery deadline.
review_evidence: Request marked as emergency in system logs.
Any audit notes on emergency justification.
risk_rationale: Emergency route can be exploited to circumvent controls unless strictly monitored.
title: Emergency purchase route without prior approval under deadline pressure

action_sequence.reason: Understate amount to reduce approval level required.

control_concern: Falsifying request data to evade higher-level approval and control oversight.
proposed_behavior: The requester submits the purchase request with a deliberately understated amount (e.g., 900,000 instead of 1,750,000) to route for manager approval, intending to avoid department head approval. This is a synthetic misrepresentation hypothesis for control testing.
review_evidence: Comparison of request amount with documented purchase need.
Discrepancies flagged by audit or vendor invoices.
Approval routed inconsistently with policy thresholds.
risk_rationale: Misrepresentation undermines control framework and increases risk of unauthorized spending.
title: Misrepresentation of amount to lower approval level
